title: Parse Syslog for Interface Flaps

problem.statement: Given raw syslog text with lines like '%LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/3, changed state to down', fill in the loop body so find_flapping_interfaces tallies state changes per interface using the already-matched regex groups, ready to be filtered by threshold.
problem.use_case: Flap-counting from raw syslog text is the building block behind 'which interfaces are unstable' dashboards — instead of a human scrolling thousands of log lines, a script flags anything crossing a flap threshold in seconds.

hints: match.group(1) is the interface name captured by the regex; match.group(2) is 'up' or 'down' (not needed for the count itself).
Tally occurrences per interface name in the counts dict, the same get(key, 0) + 1 pattern as a basic counter.

solution.explanation: Extract the interface name from the match with match.group(1), then tally it: counts[iface] = counts.get(iface, 0) + 1. The final line filters interfaces whose count meets or exceeds the threshold.
